UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents an authenticated user within the FinancePal Pro application. This entity stores core user information required for personalization and linking other financial data.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

email.description: The email address of the user, primarily used for identification and communication.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was initially created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the user profile was updated.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Represents a classification tag for financial transactions, allowing users to organize their income and expenses.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

userId.description: Reference to UserProfile. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Category). Identifies which user owns this category, enabling personalized budgeting.

type.description: Specifies whether this category is for 'income' or 'expense' transactions.

iconName.description: Optional name or identifier for an icon associated with the category, used for UI representation.

Transaction.title: Transaction
Transaction.description: Represents a single financial entry (either an income or an expense) recorded by a user as part of their budget tracking.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Transaction entity.

userId.description: Reference to UserProfile. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Transaction). Links the transaction to its owning user.

amount.description: The monetary value of the transaction. This value is always positive, with the 'type' field indicating if it's income or expense.

description.description: A brief textual description or memo providing details about the transaction.

date.description: The calendar date on which the transaction occurred.

type.description: The type of the financial transaction, either 'income' or 'expense'.

categoryId.description: Reference to Category. (Relationship: Category 1:N Transaction). Specifies the classification for this transaction.

definition.description: Stores core profile information for each authenticated user. The `id` field within the document must match the `{userId}` path parameter, ensuring direct ownership. This enables simple, path-based security rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Contains user-defined categories for financial transactions. Each document includes a `userId` field that must match the `{userId}` path parameter, denormalizing ownership for authorization independence and simplified security rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the owning user, corresponding to `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific category.

definition.description: Stores individual financial transactions (income or expense) for each user. Each document includes a `userId` field that must match the `{userId}` path parameter, denormalizing ownership for authorization independence and simplified security rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the owning user, corresponding to `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific transaction.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for FinancePal Pro prioritizes robust security, scalability, and debuggability by adhering strictly to the core design principles. All user-specific data is nested under a top-level `/users/{userId}` collection, where `{userId}` directly corresponds to the authenticated user's Firebase `uid` (`request.auth.uid`). This foundational approach simplifies security rules and eliminates cross-user data access issues.

**Authorization Independence:** This structure achieves authorization independence by ensuring that all user-owned documents (UserProfile, Category, Transaction) explicitly contain a `userId` field that matches the `{userId}` wildcard in their respective paths. For example, a `Category` document at `/users/{userId}/categories/{categoryId}` will have its `userId` field set to the same `userId` as in the path. This denormalization means that security rules can evaluate access requests by simply comparing `request.auth.uid` with the `{userId}` wildcard in the path (or the `userId` field in the document), without needing to perform expensive and dependency-creating `get()` operations on parent documents. This allows for atomic writes and updates across user-owned collections.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** The structure inherently supports Query as a Principal (QAPs) by strictly segregating data per user. 
*   For `UserProfile`, a user can only read or write their own profile document located at `/users/{request.auth.uid}`. There is no `list` operation for user profiles other than the authenticated user's own.
*   For `Category` and `Transaction` entities, a user can securely `list` their own data by querying their specific subcollections, i.e., `/users/{request.auth.uid}/categories` and `/users/{request.auth.uid}/transactions`. The security rules will be designed to only allow `list` operations where the path's `{userId}` wildcard matches `request.auth.uid`, ensuring that users can only retrieve data belonging to them. This prevents accidental data leakage and ensures that client-side queries are inherently secure without relying on post-query filtering.
